concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: Manifest plus complete titles and available abstracts for all 150 ordered candidate records in CONCEPT-BATCH-003-A2-SLICE-007_RECORDS.csv, compared against the single provisional concept row in CONCEPT-BATCH-003-A2-SLICE-007_CONCEPTS.csv.
concept_decisions.rationale: The provisional concept is a residual bucket rather than a design-coherent concept. The 150 records separate into five recurring design types: descriptive surveillance analyses, hidden-burden estimation, surveillance and reporting-system evaluation, alternative surveillance data streams, and partial-observation epidemic inference. Splitting removes the vague fallback while preserving one family-level design domain.

new_concepts.rationale: For studies that mainly characterize incidence, prevalence, trends, hotspots, or burden patterns from surveillance, administrative, review, or cohort data without making hidden-burden correction the central task.
For studies that mainly estimate true incidence, prevalence, hospitalization, mortality, or economic burden by explicitly correcting for underreporting or under-ascertainment.

new_concepts.canonical_name: Surveillance system evaluation and reporting improvement
new_concepts.rationale: For studies focused on reporting completeness, misclassification, surveillance infrastructure, pharmacovigilance or notification barriers, governance, or interventions and guidance that improve reporting performance.

For studies centered on enhanced or nonroutine measurement streams, including wastewater, serosurveillance, screening proxies, genomic surveillance archives, participatory surveillance, or digital trace data.
For studies whose central contribution is methodological inference or forecasting when surveillance is incomplete, noisy, delayed, zero-inflated, or otherwise only partially observed.
